_comment: Single source of truth for the desktop-agent performance budgets (Desktop Agent plan T7.1; spec docs/product/desktop-agent-spec.md section 21). The evidence doc, the CI artifact-size gate (scripts/desktop-artifact-size-gate.mjs), and the harness (scripts/desktop-perf-harness.mjs) ALL read these numbers so they can never drift. Numbers are copied verbatim from the spec section 21 table. `measuredBy` says HOW each budget is checked today: `ci-artifact-size` runs automatically on every desktop PR; `on-hardware` still needs a human to run the documented procedure on real macOS/Windows hardware (not yet automated, not yet measured).

installedSize.label: Installed size (per architecture)
installedSize.note: The CI gate applies this budget to the produced installer/bundle file(s) (.dmg / .msi / .exe) on each build runner. Installers are compressed, so an installer over 40 MB proves the installed app is over budget; an installer under 40 MB is a necessary-but-not-sufficient signal. The true unpacked installed size still needs the on-hardware step below.

idleMemory.label: Idle memory (resident set)
idleMemory.note: Resident memory of the tray process after 10 minutes idle, no window open.

idleCpu.label: Idle CPU, averaged over 10 minutes
idleCpu.note: Average CPU of the agent process over a 10-minute idle window.

activeCpu.label: Active CPU, averaged over 1 minute
activeCpu.note: Average CPU over a 1-minute window while a sync is running.

startupToTray.label: Startup to tray ready
startupToTray.note: Time from launching the app to the tray icon being interactive.

warmWindowOpen.label: Warm status-window open
warmWindowOpen.note: Time to show the status window on a second/warm open (app already resident).

idleNetwork.label: Idle network use (excluding updates)
idleNetwork.note: Total bytes sent+received over 24 hours idle, not counting update downloads.
